reason_for_decision: The article passes because it compares AOA, TDOA, and FDOA through geometry, synchronization, and signal behavior instead of treating them as generic interchangeable geolocation features.
strengths: The article is strong at identifying geometry and synchronization as the real selection drivers, which gives the comparison practical engineering value.
It avoids false certainty by explaining where each method becomes weak rather than presenting one as universally superior.
The hybrid-geolocation discussion helps the reader understand why real RF projects often combine methods instead of forcing a single-mode design.
summary_assessment: This is a strong RF geolocation comparison article. It is useful, technically aware, and well scoped for system planners choosing between locating methods.
